name: ML Flood Detection and Mapping from Satellite Imagery
description: Interventions using deep learning and remote sensing (SAR, optical, radar) to detect, segment, and map flood extents, often in near-real-time for disaster response. Includes multimodal fusion, crowdsourcing integration, and handling of cloudy or occluded scenes. Spans model development, deployment for early warning, and evaluation against operational needs.

name: Data-Driven Streamflow and Rainfall-Runoff Modeling
description: Research applying neural networks (GNNs, RNNs, domain adaptation) to predict river discharge, streamflow, and hydrological response in catchments, with emphasis on data-sparse regions like West Africa. Includes imputation of missing gauge data and generalization across basins. Central to water resource management and flood forecasting.

name: Drought Forecasting and Impact Monitoring
description: Interventions combining climate/satellite data with ML to forecast drought severity and duration, classify agricultural drought, and detect atmospheric drivers. Extends to social-signal approaches (Twitter, impact reporters) for measuring on-the-ground drought impacts. Spans dataset construction, model development, and impact assessment.

name: Weather and Climate Model Emulation and Downscaling
description: Machine learning surrogates and foundation models that emulate physical climate/weather models, correct biases, super-resolve outputs, and downscale to local scales. Includes diffusion models, calibration, and uncertainty quantification for climate projections. Primarily technical but tied to actionable regional climate information.

name: Extreme Weather Event Detection and Forecasting
description: ML systems for detecting and forecasting tropical cyclones, hail, extreme precipitation, and hydrological extremes from meteorological and satellite data. Includes lightweight/efficient models for operational deployment and correction of reanalysis products. Focused on early warning and disaster preparedness.

name: Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segmentation
description: Deep learning for semantic segmentation and classification of land use/land cover, water bodies, aquaculture ponds, and irrigation from remote sensing imagery. Emphasizes methods for label scarcity (few-shot, self-supervised, synthetic data, multiple-instance learning) and cross-sensor transfer. Underpins many downstream water and environmental monitoring applications.

name: Social Media and Crowdsourced Disaster Response Analytics
description: Using NLP and deep learning on social media (Twitter) and crowdsourced data to analyze disaster narratives, community needs, and event impacts during cyclones, floods, and droughts. Bridges computational methods with human experience and humanitarian response. Spans model development, impact evaluation, and communication.
